title: Epic: Deploy Wave to Contabo behind Cloudflare
description: Plan and implement automated deployment of Wave to the Contabo Linux host and wire public DNS for supawave.ai through Cloudflare.

comments.author: Codex
comments.text:
Deployment plan review round 1:
- Reviewer confirmed the original plan was only a discovery outline, not implementation-ready.
- Updated the plan to choose Docker Compose + Caddy as the default topology, define the canonical hostname (`wave.supawave.ai`), spell out secret ownership, add rollback steps, split GitHub Actions vs host responsibilities, and add a post-deploy verification checklist.
- Explicitly documented the current Cloudflare blocker: `supawave.ai` is not visible in the current token context yet, so zone ownership/onboarding must be verified before DNS automation can complete.

comments.author: Yuri Z.
comments.text:
Worktree: /Users/vega/devroot/worktrees/incubator-wave/deploy-supawave-contabo
Branch: deploy-supawave-contabo
Lead: Codex worker

Implementation direction:
- Ship the Wave image over SSH from GitHub Actions.
- Run the host as a Docker Compose stack with Caddy terminating public traffic.
- Keep Wave itself internal to the compose network and persist data under the deploy root.
- Reuse the existing Gradle installDist / smoke path as the preflight check before packaging the image.

comments.author: Yuri Z.
comments.text:
Implementation commit: 8a885dad Add Contabo deployment automation

What landed:
- Added .github/workflows/deploy-contabo.yml to build the Wave image, ship a release bundle over SSH, and run the host deploy script.
- Added deploy/contabo/{compose.yml,Caddyfile,application.conf,deploy.sh} for the Docker Compose rollout, Caddy reverse proxy, runtime config, and rollback path.
- Added docs/deployment/contabo.md to document the topology, secrets, rollout, and rollback assumptions.

Verification:
- bash -n deploy/contabo/deploy.sh
- ruby -e 'require "yaml"; YAML.load_file(".github/workflows/deploy-contabo.yml") ; puts "workflow-yaml-ok"'
- DEPLOY_ROOT=/tmp/supawave WAVE_IMAGE=test-image CANONICAL_HOST=wave.supawave.ai ROOT_HOST=supawave.ai WWW_HOST=www.supawave.ai WAVE_INTERNAL_PORT=9898 docker compose -f deploy/contabo/compose.yml config
- docker run --rm -e CANONICAL_HOST=wave.supawave.ai -e ROOT_HOST=supawave.ai -e WWW_HOST=www.supawave.ai -e WAVE_INTERNAL_PORT=9898 -v /Users/vega/devroot/worktrees/incubator-wave/deploy-supawave-contabo/deploy/contabo/Caddyfile:/etc/caddy/Caddyfile:ro caddy:2.8.4-alpine caddy validate --config /etc/caddy/Caddyfile --adapter caddyfile

Topology rationale:
- Docker Compose on the Contabo host is the narrowest practical runtime because Docker is already present, nginx/cloudflared are absent, and the release bundle can stay self-contained.
- Caddy on the host side keeps HTTPS and apex/www redirects simple while Wave stays internal to the compose network.
- The deployment stays SSH-driven from GitHub Actions, so rollback is just a release pointer flip plus compose restart.

comments.author: Codex
comments.text:
PR: https://github.com/vega113/incubator-wave/pull/12

Branch state:
- deploy-supawave-contabo now contains the reviewed deployment plan, Cloudflare DNS prep, and the Contabo GitHub Actions + Docker Compose rollout bundle.
- Cloudflare live DNS remains blocked on zone visibility; no external DNS changes were applied from this branch.

title: Automate GitHub Actions deployment to the Contabo host
description: Bootstrap a reproducible runtime on the Contabo server and add a GitHub Actions workflow that deploys Wave over SSH.

comments.author: Codex
comments.text: Addressing the current CodeRabbit feedback on deploy/contabo/deploy.sh and docs/deployment/contabo.md: fail the deploy function explicitly after rollback so CI does not report a rolled-back release as successful, and join the rollback sentence so the docs read cleanly. I will verify the focused changes and record the commit SHA in a follow-up Beads comment.

comments.author: Codex
comments.text:
Implementation commit: 80d77b60 Fix Contabo rollback failure signaling

What changed:
- Made deploy/contabo/deploy.sh exit non-zero after rollback so a failed rollout no longer reports success.
- Joined the rollback sentence in docs/deployment/contabo.md so the manual rollback instructions read cleanly.

Focused verification:
- bash -n deploy/contabo/deploy.sh
- git diff --check
- rg -n "Smoke checks failed, rolling back|release directory" deploy/contabo/deploy.sh docs/deployment/contabo.md

Review follow-up:
- Addressed CodeRabbit's two actionable comments on deploy/contabo/deploy.sh and docs/deployment/contabo.md exactly as requested.
- No broader deployment topology changes were made in this follow-up.

title: Configure Cloudflare DNS for supawave.ai
description: Set up the public DNS records for the Wave deployment using Cloudflare and capture the operational assumptions for the chosen hostnames.

comments.author: Codex
comments.text:
Implementation commit: db045842 Add Cloudflare DNS prep for supawave.ai

What changed:
- Added scripts/cloudflare/supawave-dns.sh to verify the Cloudflare zone and apply the minimal apex A record when the zone becomes available.
- Added docs/deployment/cloudflare-supawave.md with the blocked-zone finding, exact onboarding steps, and the no-live-change outcome.

Verification:
- npx wrangler whoami (token recognized)
- Cloudflare zones query for supawave.ai returned success=true with result=[] and total_count=0
- ./scripts/cloudflare/supawave-dns.sh exits 2 with the blocked-zone message when the zone is absent

External changes:
- None. No DNS records were created because supawave.ai is absent from the accessible Cloudflare account context.

title: Document deploy topology, secrets, and rollback
description: Document the chosen runtime topology, required GitHub/Cloudflare secrets, and the rollback path so future deployments are operationally safe.

title: Epic: Establish canonical Wave resumption docs and Beads backlog
description:
## Issue
The repository accumulated several planning and review documents during the modernization and Wiab import work. Some of them are now stale or contradictory, which makes resuming work slower than it should be.

## Goal
Create one canonical resume document, retire stale docs, initialize repo-local Beads tracking, and record the next-phase roadmap in epics and tasks.

## Plan
1. Add a canonical current-state document.
2. Update README and the surviving detailed ledgers to point at the canonical docs.
3. Remove stale one-off documents once their findings are folded into the canonical set.
4. Initialize repo-local Beads tracking in no-db mode and create epics/tasks for the remaining work.

## DoD
- `docs/current-state.md` exists and is the canonical resume document.
- README points to the canonical doc set.
- Stale planning/review documents are removed or clearly superseded.
- `.beads/issues.jsonl` contains the epics and tasks for the remaining work.
acceptance_criteria:
- Canonical doc set is easy to discover from `README.md`.
- Repo-local Beads backlog exists and is git-tracked.
- No stale review-only or superseded planning docs remain in the main docs surface.
close_reason: Canonical docs, Beads backlog, and stale-doc cleanup completed

title: Epic: Keep canonical Wave docs and Beads synchronized
description:
## Issue
The repository has one canonical resume guide now, but the remaining long-form ledgers still need to be kept in sync with the live Beads backlog so future work does not restart from stale assumptions.

## Goal
Keep the canonical docs map aligned with the live backlog, retire one-off planning artifacts, and document the current workspace conventions so the next agent does not need to rediscover them.

## Plan
1. Refresh the canonical docs map in README and the resume guide.
2. Retire or reclassify stale long-form docs as historical references.
3. Record the shared worktree root preference and keep Beads epics/tasks aligned with the docs surface.

## DoD
- README and docs/current-state.md point at the same canonical doc set.
- Historical ledgers are explicitly labeled as references, not source of truth.
- One-off planning artifacts are removed once their content is folded into the canonical docs.
- The worktree root preference is recorded in the repo instructions.
acceptance_criteria:
- The docs map is consistent across README, docs/current-state.md, and docs/epics/README.md.
- Stale one-off plan docs are removed or clearly marked historical.
- The repository instructions capture the shared worktree root preference.
- Beads remains the live task tracker for all remaining roadmap work.
close_reason: Completed by the 2026-03-18 docs maintenance refresh

title: Epic: Replace legacy OAuth flows with JWT-based service authentication
description: Design and deliver a modern replacement for the legacy OAuth 1.0 robot, Data API, and gadget authorization model. The short-term goal is to remove obsolete OAuth dependencies safely; the follow-on goal is to define a JWT-based design that can restore needed automation capabilities on a modern, supportable security model.
notes: Canonical design docs live under docs/JWT/. Initial research lane worktree: /Users/vega/devroot/worktrees/incubator-wave/jwt-auth-research-lane

comments.author: Codex
comments.text: JWT replacement lane opened. Research scope covers robot request signing, Data API authorization, and the existing gadget/OpenSocial OAuth helper path. Removal of the legacy OAuth runtime path is being handled in parallel on the remove-legacy-oauth worktree under incubator-wave-modernization.3.

title: Write the JWT replacement architecture and migration plan
description: Investigate current OAuth/robot/gadget auth surfaces, choose the target JWT model, document token validation rules and rollout phases, and record the reviewed plan under docs/JWT/.

comments.author: Codex
comments.text:
Plan review round 1 (Claude):
- Accepted the overall JWT bearer design, migration sequence, and robot/Data API/gadget boundary split.
- Required follow-ups: enumerate the missing OAuth-coupled files (BaseApiServlet, OAuthUtil, expimp/OAuth, WaveService, OAuthUser, OpenSocial transport helpers, SimpleLoginFormHandler), make the Jakarta override tree explicit as the live runtime path, and assign target owners/deadlines for the remaining design decisions.
- Canonical plan updated at docs/JWT/2026-03-19-jwt-auth-redesign-plan.md and companion inventory retained at docs/JWT/2026-03-19-jwt-auth-code-surface-inventory.md.
- Next: rerun the review after those updates and continue until there are no actionable comments.

comments.author: Codex
comments.text:
Plan review round 2 (Claude):
- Accepted the architecture after the surface-enumeration fixes, but required more plan precision around browser/Data API claim shapes, CSRF for cookie-carried JWTs, validator placement, and the refresh/session-renewal decision.
- Also required the Beads backlog to match the implementation slicing described in the plan.
- Next: tighten the plan details and add the missing backlog tasks before rerunning review.

comments.author: Codex
comments.text:
Plan review round 3 (Claude):
- Architecture is now considered conditionally approved.
- Remaining required follow-ups are administrative, not conceptual: update the companion inventory with the remaining OAuth helper files and align the Beads task layout with the plan's execution slices before closing this planning task.
- Next: apply those two cleanups and rerun once more until there are no actionable comments.

comments.author: Codex
comments.text:
Plan review round 4 (Claude):
- Reviewer confirmed the remaining issues were mechanical consistency problems only.
- Follow-ups applied: fixed the lingering gadget-task id mismatch in the plan, added `.2` as a blocking dependency for implementation tasks `.3` through `.6`, and introduced an explicit Phase 6 cleanup task in the Beads backlog.
- Next: rerun once more to confirm there are no actionable comments left.

comments.author: Codex
comments.text:
Plan review round 5 (Claude):
- Reviewer found one remaining actionable item: `incubator-wave-jwt-auth.7` needed the browser/WebSocket migration task `.3` as a blocking dependency, because Phase 6 cleanup must also wait for the browser auth rollout.
- Applied that dependency update in Beads.
- Next: rerun once more for a no-actionable-comments result.

comments.author: Codex
comments.text:
Plan review round 6 (Claude):
- Final result: pass.
- The reviewer confirmed the plan, companion inventory, and Beads task graph have no remaining actionable comments.
- Residual notes are non-blocking polish only, not gating work: task-comment drafts are richer than the current Beads descriptions, persistence/schema cleanup is split across `.4` and `.7`, and the markdown links should eventually be made relative before or during merge cleanup.
- This satisfies the requirement to keep iterating review until there are no actionable comments.

comments.author: Codex
comments.text:
PR: https://github.com/vega113/incubator-wave/pull/14

This PR contains the canonical JWT redesign plan, companion code-surface inventory, Beads epic/task graph, and the fully recorded Claude review loop that ended in a pass with no actionable comments.

comments.author: Codex
comments.text:
Review follow-up for PR #14:
- Commit: 301064bc docs: fix JWT review nits
- Fixed the compound modifier in docs/JWT/2026-03-19-jwt-auth-redesign-plan.md.
- Converted the JWT inventory links to repository-relative targets so the markdown works outside this worktree.
- Verified with git diff and rg that the inventory no longer contains the /Users/vega/devroot/worktrees/incubator-wave/jwt-auth-research-lane prefix.

title: Build the shared JWT issuer and validator foundation
description: Add the shared JWT platform: key management/JWKS publication, validator abstraction, token types, audience/scope rules, and the common enforcement contract used by HTTP and WebSocket auth.

comments.author: Yuri Z.
comments.text:
Worktree: /Users/vega/devroot/worktrees/incubator-wave/jwt-shared-platform
Branch: jwt-shared-platform
Owner: incubator-wave-jwt-auth.2

Scope for the first slice:
- add the shared JWT foundation in a reusable package under wave/src/main/java/org/waveprotocol/box/server/authentication/jwt
- keep endpoint behavior unchanged for now
- define token types, claims, issuer/validator contracts, and revocation/version metadata so browser, WebSocket, robot, Data API, and gadget auth can converge later

Planned owned files for this slice:
- wave/src/main/java/org/waveprotocol/box/server/authentication/jwt/JwtTokenType.java
- wave/src/main/java/org/waveprotocol/box/server/authentication/jwt/JwtAudience.java
- wave/src/main/java/org/waveprotocol/box/server/authentication/jwt/JwtClaims.java
- wave/src/main/java/org/waveprotocol/box/server/authentication/jwt/JwtIssuer.java
- wave/src/main/java/org/waveprotocol/box/server/authentication/jwt/JwtValidator.java
- wave/src/main/java/org/waveprotocol/box/server/authentication/jwt/JwtTokenContext.java
- wave/src/main/java/org/waveprotocol/box/server/authentication/jwt/JwtRevocationState.java
- focused unit tests for the new shared contract package

comments.author: Yuri Z.
comments.text:
Implementation approach for the next slice:
- use RS256 compact JWTs
- keep the key ring in-memory for now, with RSA key pairs identified by kid
- publish JWKS from the public keys in that ring
- use only JDK crypto + the repo's existing Gson dependency for JSON encoding
- keep endpoint wiring untouched until the shared crypto path is proven in tests

Owned files for this slice:
- wave/src/main/java/org/waveprotocol/box/server/authentication/jwt/JwtKeyMaterial.java
- wave/src/main/java/org/waveprotocol/box/server/authentication/jwt/JwtKeyRing.java
- wave/src/main/java/org/waveprotocol/box/server/authentication/jwt/JwtValidationException.java
- wave/src/main/java/org/waveprotocol/box/server/authentication/jwt/RsaJwtIssuer.java
- wave/src/main/java/org/waveprotocol/box/server/authentication/jwt/RsaJwtValidator.java
- wave/src/test/java/org/waveprotocol/box/server/authentication/jwt/RsaJwtIssuerValidatorTest.java
- wave/src/test/java/org/waveprotocol/box/server/authentication/jwt/JwtKeyRingTest.java

comments.author: Yuri Z.
comments.text:
Scope update from product direction:
- gadget/OpenSocial is de-scoped from this milestone
- keep .2 focused on browser/WebSocket session JWTs plus robot active and Data API JWT support
- do not add gadget compatibility work or gadget-facing token types in this task

Current focus files remain the shared browser/robot/Data API JWT contracts, key ring, issuer, validator, and supporting tests

comments.author: Codex
comments.text:
Worktree: /Users/vega/devroot/worktrees/incubator-wave/jwt-shared-platform
Branch: jwt-shared-platform
Lead: Codex

Architect summary:
- Added a shared browser-session JWT profile/helper so the login path does not have to re-state claim shape in each servlet.
- Issuer now signs with the key ring's primary signing key and uses a fixed browser-session TTL.

Implementation commits:
- 8d1597c7 feat: issue browser session JWTs on login

Verification:
- ./gradlew :wave:test --tests org.waveprotocol.box.server.rpc.AuthenticationServletTest --tests org.waveprotocol.box.server.authentication.jwt.BrowserSessionJwtTest --tests org.waveprotocol.box.server.authentication.jwt.BrowserSessionJwtIssuerTest
- ./gradlew :wave:testJakartaIT --tests org.waveprotocol.box.server.jakarta.AuthenticationServletJakartaIT

Result:
- PASS

Review:
- No separate code-review run in this slice.
- Residual risk: this is still an additive login-cookie path; browser/WebSocket consumers and handshake validation still need the phase 3 migration work.

title: Migrate browser and WebSocket auth to JWT-backed sessions
description: Replace JS-readable session-cookie auth with HttpOnly cookie-carried JWT-backed sessions and handshake-based WebSocket authentication.

title: Migrate robot auth to asymmetric JWT bearer tokens
description: Replace robot shared-secret OAuth validation with asymmetric key registration, JWT bearer access, and server-signed callback auth.

title: Migrate Data API auth to scoped JWT bearer tokens
description: Replace the Data API OAuth 1.0 request/access-token flow with scoped JWT bearer tokens and explicit audience validation.

title: Resolve gadget JWT scope or retirement
description: Decide whether gadget-facing auth remains supported and either define a JWT-backed st model or explicitly retire the gadget auth surface.

title: Remove legacy OAuth compatibility code after JWT rollout
description: After the JWT-backed browser, robot, Data API, and gadget decisions are implemented, delete the remaining compatibility-only OAuth pages, token containers, shared-secret fields, and dead helper code.

title: Epic: Complete modernization phases 6 through 8
description:
## Issue
The Wave codebase already completed the Java 17, Gradle 8, GWT 2.x baseline, and Jakarta / Jetty 12 migration work, but the post-Jakarta modernization backlog is still open.

## Goal
Finish the remaining modernization work so the project has one consistent build/config story, a complete MongoDB v4 migration path, verified packaging, and a clear post-GWT roadmap.

## Scope
- Config hygiene and flag consolidation.
- MongoDB v4 delta-store completion.
- Remaining library upgrade work.
- SBT parity and packaging / DX verification.
- J2CL / GWT 3 inventory and planning.

## DoD
- The remaining tasks in modernization phases 6 through 8 are completed or explicitly re-scoped.
- The detailed ledger in `docs/modernization-plan.md` matches the live state of the codebase.
- Follow-on product work can proceed without re-opening the infrastructure baseline.
acceptance_criteria:
- No fragment or segment configuration depends on ad-hoc `System.getProperty(...)` paths.
- MongoDB driver v4 path includes `Mongo4DeltaStore`.
- SBT docs and behavior match the actual additive build.
- Packaging and smoke docs reflect the Jakarta default.
- A J2CL / GWT 3 inventory exists.

title: Consolidate config hygiene and fragment flag paths
description: Audit and remove direct server-side `System.getProperty(...)` usage for fragment, segment-state, and related config keys; keep Typesafe Config as the single source of truth with system-property override precedence.
close_reason: Closed after aligning fragment flag resolution with the active Jakarta runtime and verifying focused Jakarta regressions

comments.author: Yuri Z.
comments.text:
Worktree: /Users/vega/devroot/worktrees/incubator-wave/config-hygiene
Branch: config-hygiene
Lead: Codex team lead
Status: intake started; architect and planner dispatch pending.

comments.author: Yuri Z.
comments.text:
Architect summary:
- Root cause: fragment/segment-state config drift caused by mixing injected Config with ConfigFactory.load() and System.getProperty(...) in server paths.
- Likely seams: StatuszServlet.writeFragments, WaveClientServlet.getClientFlags, ServerMain fragment transport/property mirroring.
- Plan file: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-03-18-config-hygiene-plan.md
- Next: Claude plan review, then worker TDD implementation.

comments.author: Codex
comments.text:
Implementation commit: 5c1388d9

Implementation summary:
- Removed fragment transport/property mirroring from ServerMain.
- Made StatuszServlet read the injected Config directly for transport and segment-state values.
- Reworked WaveClientServlet to derive fragment-related defaults from Config instead of ambient wave.clientFlags system properties.
- Added focused regressions for status config precedence, client fragment defaults, and removal of the transport mirror helper.

Focused verification:
./gradlew -q :wave:compileJava; javac + JUnitCore on StatuszServletConfigTest, WaveClientServletFragmentDefaultsTest, ServerMainFragmentConfigMirroringTest

Residual risk:
- The wider :wave:test task still has unrelated pre-existing test compilation failures outside this change set, so verification was limited to the touched tests and compileJava.

comments.author: Yuri Z.
comments.text:
Follow-up implementation commits:
- 0864294c Align config hygiene with Jakarta runtime
- e8e1170a Add config flag precedence regressions

What changed:
- Moved the focused verification to wave/src/jakarta-test so the task is validated on the maintained Jakarta lane rather than the broken legacy compileTestJava lane.
- Brought the active Jakarta WaveClientServlet override into parity with the intended fragment flag precedence: request params, explicit wave.clientFlags overrides, object defaults, derived fragment defaults, then CSV defaults.
- Added focused precedence regressions for object defaults beating derived fragment defaults and CSV defaults still applying when object defaults are absent.

Focused verification:
./gradlew -q :wave:testJakarta --tests org.waveprotocol.box.server.rpc.WaveClientServletFragmentDefaultsTest --tests org.waveprotocol.box.server.stat.StatuszServletConfigTest

Claude review:
- No blockers.
- Review flagged missing object-form / CSV / precedence coverage; addressed in e8e1170a.
- Review also noted the remaining structural divergence between the legacy and Jakarta WaveClientServlet implementations. That is recorded as a non-blocking follow-up because a deeper deduplication refactor is outside this task scope.

comments.author: Yuri Z.
comments.text: PR: https://github.com/vega113/incubator-wave/pull/2

title: Implement Mongo4DeltaStore and complete the v4 store set
description: Finish the MongoDB v4 migration by implementing `Mongo4DeltaStore`, wiring it behind the existing config flag, and validating it with integration tests.

title: Close remaining library-upgrade debt
description: Verify Guava, OAuth, commons, and related modernization tasks that remain after the Java 17 / Jakarta migration, and either complete or explicitly retire them.

comments.author: Yuri Z.
comments.text:
Worktree: /Users/vega/devroot/worktrees/incubator-wave/library-upgrades
Branch: library-upgrades
Lead: architect

Architect summary:
- Verified that protobuf and server-side Guava debt are already closed on the Gradle path.
- Remaining actionable debt is concentrated in legacy OAuth ownership, MongoDB 2.x runtime usage, commons multipart/CLI cleanup, and SBT parity drift.
- Found a Jakarta runtime parity gap: the default AttachmentServlet override no longer implements multipart upload while commons-fileupload 1.5 still ships on the runtime path.

Plan:
- docs/superpowers/plans/2026-03-18-library-upgrades-plan.md
- Claude plan review: completed once with actionable findings; plan updated to add Beads-task cross-references, chunk dependencies, pinned commons-cli target, and scope-audit steps for MongoDB and OAuth.

comments.author: Codex
comments.text:
Plan review follow-up:
- Updated docs/superpowers/plans/2026-03-18-library-upgrades-plan.md in 5e121986 to verify the OAuth exclusion switch before changing build logic, use :wave:itTest for Mongo v4 work, defer stale OAuth repository cleanup to Chunk 4, and remove the human-blocking product gate from the default path.
- Baseline proved that commons-cli was not explicit on the wave compile path and that -PexcludeLegacyOAuth=true did not actually remove net.oauth from either classpath before the build logic change.
- Chunk 1 is intentionally limited to docs/build ownership cleanup; Mongo implementation and multipart runtime work remain in later chunks.

comments.author: Codex
comments.text:
Chunk 2 implementation summary:
- Brought the Jakarta AttachmentServlet override to parity with the existing servlet-native multipart upload path used by the legacy servlet.
- Added multipart configuration to the Jakarta servlet registration path in ServerRpcProvider so request.getParts() works in the real embedded server, not just in unit code.
- Added focused Jakarta integration coverage for successful upload and a legacy servlet unit test for the same upload contract.
- Removed commons-fileupload from wave/build.gradle runtime dependencies.

Focused verification:
- ./gradlew -q -PjettyFamily=jakarta :wave:testJakartaIT --tests org.waveprotocol.box.server.jakarta.AttachmentServletJakartaIT
- ./gradlew -q :wave:dependencyInsight --dependency commons-fileupload --configuration runtimeClasspath

Known blocker outside this chunk:
- The legacy javax verification lane is still blocked by pre-existing compile failures in unrelated sources (for example ServerMain / FragmentsServlet tests), so the maintained Jakarta lane is the verification source of truth for this slice.

Review note:
- External review was invoked for this chunk, but the CLI review runner did not return a usable output before commit time. No local blocker was found in the final narrowed diff.

comments.author: Codex
comments.text:
Implementation commit: ff4a4185 Add Mongo4 persistence regressions

What changed:
- Added Mongo4SignerInfoStoreIT and Mongo4DeltaStoreIT, reusing the existing CertPathStoreTestBase and DeltaStoreTestBase patterns against a MongoDB 6.0 Testcontainers fixture.
- Added PersistenceModuleMongo4IT to verify the v4 driver path binds the Mongo4 signer-info, attachment, account, and delta stores.
- Updated wave/config/reference.conf to remove the stale note that v4 still left account and delta stores on legacy implementations.

Focused verification:
- ./gradlew :wave:compileJava --no-daemon
- ./gradlew :wave:compileTestJava --no-daemon --continue

Known blocker outside this slice:
- :wave:compileTestJava still fails in unrelated legacy tests, for example FragmentsHttpGatingTest, ServerMain*ValidationTest, DataApiOAuthServletTest, AttachmentServletUploadTest, and FragmentsServletViewportTest.

comments.author: Codex
comments.text:
Mongo4 follow-up after integrating the current Jakarta-only baseline:
- Merged `retire-javax-fallback` into this branch locally so the Mongo/library slice runs against the fixed `compileTestJava` baseline.
- Fixed the Jakarta AttachmentServletUploadTest drift introduced by the multipart slice so the branch compiles on the active Jakarta path.
- Fixed Mongo4DeltaStore distinct/codec handling by querying wave ids as `String` and decoding stored applied deltas from either `Binary` or raw `byte[]`.
- Updated Mongo4AccountStoreIT to use a valid salt length for the current password policy.
- Wired `itTest` to auto-set `TESTCONTAINERS_RYUK_DISABLED=true` when running against the detected Colima socket so the Mongo Testcontainers suite runs locally without extra shell setup.

Fresh verification:
- ./gradlew -q :wave:compileJava
- ./gradlew -q :wave:itTest --tests org.waveprotocol.box.server.persistence.mongodb4.Mongo4AccountStoreIT --tests org.waveprotocol.box.server.persistence.mongodb4.Mongo4AttachmentStoreIT --tests org.waveprotocol.box.server.persistence.mongodb4.Mongo4DeltaStoreIT --tests org.waveprotocol.box.server.persistence.mongodb4.Mongo4SignerInfoStoreIT --tests org.waveprotocol.box.server.persistence.mongodb4.PersistenceModuleMongo4IT

Result:
- Both commands passed on this branch after the follow-up fixes.
- The branch is materially closer to a reviewable PR; the next step is to push this integration/fix slice and decide whether any remaining OAuth/doc cleanup belongs in the same PR or a follow-up chunk.

title: Honor Jakarta websocket timeout and message-size config
description: Apply `network.websocket_max_idle_time` and `network.websocket_max_message_size` to the Jetty 12 / Jakarta websocket container so the runtime matches the documented config instead of falling back to the container's 30s idle timeout.
close_reason: Fixed by wiring the Jakarta websocket container to the existing timeout and message-size config

title: Harden browser websocket reconnect handling after disconnects
description: Review and harden the browser websocket reconnect loop so later disconnects do not silently exhaust the reconnect budget or leave the client permanently disconnected.
close_reason: Closed after hardening the reconnect loop and adding focused Jakarta regressions for reconnect edge cases

comments.author: Yuri Z.
comments.text:
Worktree: /Users/vega/devroot/worktrees/incubator-wave/websocket-reconnect
Branch: websocket-reconnect
Lead: Codex team lead (TL-websocket-reconnect)

Opening task intake:
- Task confirmed ready: blocker incubator-wave-modernization.7 is closed.
- Following docs/superpowers/plans/2026-03-18-agent-orchestration-plan.md and docs/superpowers/plans/2026-03-18-outstanding-task-batching-plan.md.
- Next: architect root-cause analysis, planner plan update, Claude plan review, worker TDD implementation, reviewer pass, PR to main.

comments.author: Yuri Z.
comments.text:
Architect summary:
- Root cause: WaveWebSocketClient spends reconnect budget while already connected and never resets/re-arms the reconnect loop after later disconnects.
- Likely seams: WaveWebSocketClient.connect, reconnectCommand.execute, onConnect, onDisconnect.
- Plan file: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-03-18-websocket-reconnect-plan.md
- Next: Claude plan review, then worker TDD implementation.

comments.author: Yuri Z.
comments.text:
Implementation and review-response commits:
- ad1e865c Harden websocket reconnect loop
- 0995cf41 Move websocket reconnect test to Jakarta lane
- 1e3d4bbd Add reconnect edge-case regressions

What changed:
- The reconnect loop now spends budget only on real connect attempts, resets reconnect state on successful connect, and re-arms reconnect scheduling after disconnect.
- Focused verification was moved onto wave/src/jakarta-test so it runs on the maintained Jakarta lane.
- Added regressions for CONNECTING no-op ticks and budget exhaustion stopping the reconnect loop.

Focused verification:
./gradlew -q :wave:testJakarta --tests org.waveprotocol.box.webclient.client.WaveWebSocketClientTest

Claude review:
- No blockers.
- Review requested explicit coverage for CONNECTING-state ticks and budget exhaustion; addressed in 1e3d4bbd.
- Remaining notes are non-blocking: the CONNECTING branch relies on the underlying transport timeout to bound a stuck in-flight attempt, and the final test lives under jakarta-test rather than src/test because that is the maintained verification lane.

title: Epic: Finish the imported Wiab renderer and fragments work
description:
## Issue
The main `incubator-wave` tree already contains most of the Wiab renderer, quasi-deletion, and fragments scaffolding, but it has not yet been fully validated or finished.

## Goal
Close the remaining gaps in the already-imported Wiab core work so the dynamic renderer and fragments path are clearly supported and testable.

## Scope
- Combined feature verification.
- Remaining public renderer entrypoints.
- Fragment transport and applier behavior.
- Blocks / segment-state follow-up decisions.

## DoD
- The combined renderer + fragments + quasi-deletion path is verified on the current branch.
- Remaining TODO-level code paths are resolved or explicitly documented.
- The canonical docs and Beads backlog accurately describe the supported transport path.
acceptance_criteria:
- Dynamic renderer, fragments, and quasi-deletion can be exercised together without relying on stale merge notes.
- `DynamicRendererImpl` no longer exposes placeholder entrypoints.
- The HTTP-vs-stream fragment path is explicitly supported and documented.
- The follow-up relationship between fragment fetch and deeper blocks adoption is documented.

title: Run combined smoke verification for dynamic renderer, fragments, and quasi deletion
description: Run the merged feature stack together and record the verified behavior so future work does not restart from assumptions.

comments.author: Yuri Z.
comments.text:
Worktree: /Users/vega/devroot/worktrees/incubator-wave/core-smoke
Branch: core-smoke
Lead: Codex architect

Architect status:
- Investigating the current combined dynamic renderer + fragments + quasi-deletion verification surface.
- Planning-only pass; no implementation changes.
- Intake note: task remains blocked by incubator-wave-wiab-core.2, incubator-wave-wiab-core.3, and incubator-wave-wiab-product.1, so the smoke plan will call out gated versus runnable verification paths.

comments.author: Yuri Z.
comments.text:
Architect update:
- Plan written to docs/superpowers/plans/2026-03-18-core-smoke-plan.md
- Claude plan review completed in three rounds. Round 1 found two plan issues (run-vs-runDev ambiguity and missing restart step between variants); round 2 found one remaining gap (missing stop step before the first variant); round 3 passed after those fixes.

Investigation findings:
- The combined surface is already wired by default in wave/config/application.conf: enableDynamicRendering=true, fragmentFetchMode=http, enableFragmentFetchViewChannel=true, enableQuasiDeletionUi=true, enableFragmentsApplier=true, forceClientFragments=true.
- StageTwo is the integration seam for the dynamic renderer, fragments requesters/applier, and quasi adapter.
- DynamicRendererImpl still has TODO public dynamicRendering(...) entrypoints.
- ClientFragmentRequester currently treats HTTP 2xx as success but does not parse/apply returned fragment payloads.
- Snapshot gating remains in effect when forceClientFragments=true, so fragment windows are additive on initial open.
- Verified executable preflight commands in this worktree: ./gradlew -q :wave:compileJava and ./gradlew -q :wave:smokeUi.
- Verified blocker: default ./gradlew :wave:test path currently fails at compileTestJava due unrelated legacy test issues (javax/jakarta mismatches and stale ServerMain.applyFragmentsConfig references).

Dependency correction:
- core.1 is the prerequisite that unblocks incubator-wave-wiab-core.2, incubator-wave-wiab-core.3, and incubator-wave-wiab-product.1. My earlier intake comment misread bd show's BLOCKS section as reverse dependencies; this comment supersedes that interpretation.

- The branch recorded the first executable smoke envelope and preserved the known blocker in the canonical docs and Beads.
- The task remains open because the browser variant sweep and deeper renderer/fragments follow-up work are still incomplete; this merge only captured the current verified smoke state.

title: Finish DynamicRenderer public entrypoints and listener validation
description: Replace the remaining `dynamicRendering(...)` TODO bodies with real behavior and add the narrow validation needed to keep the public renderer contract honest.

title: Decide and implement the canonical fragment transport path
description: Either make HTTP fragment mode fully parse and apply payloads, or explicitly reduce it to fallback behavior and declare stream mode canonical.

title: Revisit snapshot gating and storage-backed segment-state follow-ups
description: Re-open the deferred decision around snapshot gating, storage-backed segment state, and the remaining blocks-adoption work now that the merged renderer path is documented.

title: Persist read state when opening a wave from the inbox
description: Ensure that opening an existing wave from the inbox focuses a concrete blip so the normal read-state write path runs and unread counts survive reloads.
close_reason: Fixed by focusing a default blip for plain wave refs and covering the selector path with a regression test

title: Fix unread counts for unopened shared waves
description:
## Issue
When one participant adds a new blip to a shared wave, another participant may not see the unread count reflected in the left messages panel until the wave is opened. That makes unopened-wave digests incorrect and hides unread work.

## Goal
Make the unopened left-panel digest compute unread counts correctly for shared waves, using the same persisted read-state model that should survive reloads.

## Plan
1. Verify the shared-wave unread reproduction against the server-side search/digest path.
2. Add a regression test around the search/digest flow for shared waves with persisted user-data read state.
3. Fix the narrowest root cause in the unopened-wave unread pipeline.
4. Re-run focused tests and document the result in Beads comments.

## DoD
- Shared waves show correct unread counts in the left panel before being opened.
- Reload preserves the same unread result.
- A regression test covers the server/client path chosen for the fix.
close_reason: Fixed in f8a34aab and covered by focused regression tests

comments.author: Yuri Z.
comments.text:
Plan prepared in docs/superpowers/plans/2026-03-18-unopened-shared-wave-unread-plan.md

Plan commit: d1f05e4e Plan unopened shared-wave unread fix

Architect finding: unopened-wave unread digests were identifying the viewer user-data wavelet by creator equality instead of user-data wavelet id ownership.

Claude plan review was attempted twice and blocked by provider overload (HTTP 529), not by a technical finding in the plan. Proceeded with the architect + planner outputs.

comments.author: Yuri Z.
comments.text:
Implementation commit: f8a34aab Fix unopened shared-wave unread counts

Implementation summary:
- Fixed unopened-wave unread digest logic to identify the viewer user-data wavelet by wavelet-id ownership instead of creator equality.
- Added regression coverage in wave/src/jakarta-test/java/org/waveprotocol/box/server/waveserver/UnreadSharedWaveDigestTest.java, including a digest-level unread-count assertion.

Focused verification:
./gradlew -q :wave:testJakarta --tests org.waveprotocol.box.server.waveserver.UnreadSharedWaveDigestTest --tests org.waveprotocol.wave.client.wavepanel.impl.focus.FocusBlipSelectorTest --tests org.waveprotocol.box.server.rpc.ServerRpcProviderWebSocketConfigTest --tests org.waveprotocol.box.server.jakarta.WaveWebSocketEndpointInitGuardTest

Review notes:
- Claude implementation review reported no material findings.
- Minor nits only: jakarta-test location, JUnit3 style, and optional extra negative/null UDW coverage.
- Addressed the main coverage concern by adding a digest-level unread-count regression, not just helper-level assertions.

title: Fix unread digests for unopened multi-wavelet shared waves
description: When a participant adds a new blip to a shared wave, the recipient can see the unread blip after opening the wave, but the unopened left inbox panel may still show the wave as fully read. Fix the unopened digest path so login-time and unopened-wave unread counts match the opened-wave state.
close_reason: Merged in PR #7 after restoring server-side unread digest aggregation across conversational wavelets and verifying the login-time inbox search path.

comments.author: Codex
comments.text:
Worktree: /Users/vega/devroot/worktrees/incubator-wave/unread-panel-live-update
Branch: unread-panel-live-update
Lead: Codex team lead

Initial investigation:
- Repro: unopened inbox row shows no unread state for a shared wave, but opening the wave shows one unread blip.
- Current strongest hypothesis: server-side WaveDigester still counts unread state from only one conversational wavelet, while the opened wave uses the full wave model.
- The repeated `WebSocket session not open` send failures look like stale-session noise unless a focused regression proves they affect persisted unread state.

Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-03-19-unread-panel-live-update-plan.md

comments.author: Codex
comments.text:
Architect review:
- Root cause is the server-side digest path, not websocket reconnect handling.
- Login-time inbox rows come from /search and static digest snapshots; opening the wave switches the row to the live WaveBasedDigest built from the full opened wave.
- The repeated `WebSocket session not open` warnings were investigated and treated as stale-session noise for this task, not as the unread-count root cause.

Implementation commit: 2347ac79 Fix unread digests for unopened multi-wavelet waves

Implementation summary:
- WaveDigester now keeps title/snippet behavior anchored to the main conversation but counts unread and total blips across every conversational wavelet in the wave view.
- Added a regression that reproduces the unopened unread mismatch when the unread blip lives on a secondary conversational wavelet.

Focused verification:
- ./gradlew -q :wave:testJakarta --tests org.waveprotocol.box.server.waveserver.UnreadSharedWaveDigestTest --tests org.waveprotocol.wave.client.wavepanel.impl.focus.FocusBlipSelectorTest

comments.author: Codex
comments.text: PR: https://github.com/vega113/incubator-wave/pull/7

comments.author: Codex
comments.text:
Recovery note:
- Commit bd383709 preserved the PR link but unintentionally reverted WaveDigester.java.
- Restored the WaveDigester fix on top of the existing branch state instead of rewriting history, so the task-history and PR-link comments stay intact.
- Also fixed the WaveDigesterTest call sites for the new generateDigest signature and tightened the new regression to assert the root title remains canonical.

Focused verification after recovery:
- ./gradlew -q :wave:testJakarta --tests org.waveprotocol.box.server.waveserver.UnreadSharedWaveDigestTest --tests org.waveprotocol.wave.client.wavepanel.impl.focus.FocusBlipSelectorTest
- ./gradlew -q :wave:test --tests org.waveprotocol.box.server.waveserver.WaveDigesterTest (still blocked by unrelated pre-existing compileTestJava failures outside this task, but the WaveDigesterTest signature mismatch from this change is addressed)

comments.author: Codex
comments.text:
Merged: https://github.com/vega113/incubator-wave/pull/7

Post-merge verification:
- Fresh vega login against the unread-fix server returned /search?query=in:inbox with wave local.net/w+Bcmv6r8hC7A at unread count 1 before any open request.
- This matches the left-panel data source and closes the original user-visible regression.

Notes:
- CodeRabbit review finished with non-blocking nits only.
- The stale `WebSocket session not open` warnings were investigated and not required for the unread fix path.

title: Epic: Evaluate and import remaining Wiab-only product features
description:
## Issue
A large part of the core renderer and transport work was imported, but several recognizable Wiab product features never made it into the active Apache Wave tree.

## Goal
Decide which of the remaining Wiab features should be imported, in what order, and under what acceptance criteria.

## Scope
- Tags, archive, and stored searches.
- Draft mode.
- Contacts.
- The deeper snapshot / history / blocks data layer.

## DoD
- The remaining Wiab-only features are either scheduled with explicit tasks or intentionally rejected with written rationale.
- Product-facing imports happen after the underlying core renderer / fragments path is verified.
acceptance_criteria:
- Each major remaining Wiab feature has an explicit audit and go/no-go decision.
- Product features do not reopen infrastructure assumptions that should be solved in the core epic.
- The backlog makes the difference between product UX imports and deep data-layer imports explicit.

title: Audit tags, archive, and stored-search features for import
description: Compare the remaining user-facing tags/archive/search functionality in `Wiab.pro` against the current Apache Wave tree and define the minimum viable import sequence.

title: Evaluate and port draft mode
description: Assess the Wiab draft-mode workflow and determine whether it can be imported cleanly into the current editor and wavepanel stack.

title: Evaluate and port contacts storage and UI
description: Assess the Wiab contacts feature set end-to-end, including stores, RPC endpoints, and UI integration, before deciding on the import scope.

title: Make a go/no-go decision on the deeper Wiab data layer
description: Decide whether the snapshot/history/blocks storage model from `Wiab.pro` should be adopted, partially mined for ideas, or explicitly left out of scope.
